content.full_text:
f

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 23, 1993

Charles P. Scully, II
Carroll & Scully, Inc.
300 Montgomery Street, Suite 735
San Francisco, CA 94104-1902

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-93-310

Dear Mr. Scully:

You have requested informal advice on behalf the United Food
and Commercial Workers Local 1288 Political Action Committee
("Local 1288 PAC") concerning the campaign reporting provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

This letter confirms telephone advice I provided to you on
July 27, 1993, and along with the following clarifying
information, confirms that the procedures outlined in your letter
of August 11, 1993, are correct. Under the Act, the United Food
and Commercial Workers Local 1288 ("Local 1288") qualifies as an
intermediary because its member contributions are first deposited
into Local 1288's bank account and later transferred into the
Local 1288 PAC bank account. (Regulation 18432.5.)

For disclosure purposes, if a transfer of $100 or more in
member contributions is received by the committee, it must
indicate on its campaign statement (Form 450 or Form 420) that
Local 1288 is acting as an intermediary. (Regulation
18419(c)(3).) In addition, any contributor of $100 or more in a
calendar year must be itemized on the statement. (Section 84211.)
You indicated that members will be making contributions of
less than $25 per calendar year, for clarification purposes, we
recommend that a notation be made on the statement that no single
contributor is required to be itemized.

Also, enclosed for your reference is Regulation 18421.1 which
defines when a contribution is "received". The regulation
provides disclosure standards for committees that receive
contributions by means of membership dues or payroll deductions.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

I-93-310
Page 2


If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact
me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Lynda Cassady
Assistant Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

sections.conclusion: Under the Act, the United Food and Commercial Workers Local 1288 qualifies as an intermediary. For disclosure purposes, if a transfer of $100 or more in member contributions is received by the committee, it must indicate on its campaign statement (Form 450 or Form 420) that Local 1288 is acting as an intermediary. Any contributor of $100 or more in a calendar year must be itemized on the statement. Since members will be making contributions of less than $25 per calendar year, the procedures outlined in the requester's letter of August 11, 1993, are correct, with a notation recommended that no single contributor is required to be itemized.
sections.question_synthetic: How should the United Food and Commercial Workers Local 1288 Political Action Committee report campaign contributions received through an intermediary where individual member contributions are less than $25 per calendar year?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Local 1288 qualifies as an intermediary for campaign reporting purposes. Transfers of $100 or more must be disclosed with notation that Local 1288 is an intermediary, and any single contributor of $100 or more must be itemized; a notation should indicate that no single contributor meets the itemization threshold when members contribute less than $25 per year.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present; question and conclusion synthesized from letter body. Formal advice letter format with clear legal analysis. Confirms prior telephone advice from July 27, 1993.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should the United Food and Commercial Workers Local 1288 Political Action Committee report campaign contributions received through an intermediary where individual member contributions are less than $25 per calendar year?

CONCLUSION: Under the Act, the United Food and Commercial Workers Local 1288 qualifies as an intermediary. For disclosure purposes, if a transfer of $100 or more in member contributions is received by the committee, it must indicate on its campaign statement (Form 450 or Form 420) that Local 1288 is acting as an intermediary. Any contributor of $100 or more in a calendar year must be itemized on the statement. Since members will be making contributions of less than $25 per calendar year, the procedures outlined in the requester's letter of August 11, 1993, are correct, with a notation recommended that no single contributor is required to be itemized.

Under the Act, the United Food and Commercial Workers Local 1288 ("Local 1288") qualifies as an intermediary because its member contributions are first deposited into Local 1288's bank account and later transferred into the Local 1288 PAC bank account. embedding.summary: FPPC confirms informal advice regarding campaign reporting procedures for the United Food and Commercial Workers Local 1288 PAC, clarifying intermediary status and disclosure requirements for member contributions under the Political Reform Act.
